description: Canonical test vectors for the SEP Democracy Soroban contract (`contracts/sep-democracy`). This file is the single source of truth for the contract's ABI: every error code, enum value, validation boundary, wire shape, dispatch routing, and bitmap-to-field-element packing rule below MUST match the contract's source. The `test_vectors_consistency` inline test loads this file and asserts the contract's `Error` enum, `tier_capacity`, and IC-point constants match these values byte-for-byte. Vectors that depend on Poseidon / Groth16 outputs are deliberately omitted: those are produced by the Phase A `prove_democracy_v2` reference implementation (per `docs/democracy-update-testnet-design.md` §6) and added to a future `cross-platform-test-vectors.json` extension. The vectors here cover the contract surface only.
architecture_note: This contract is the per-type Democracy contract — one of the eventual four (Anarchy, OneOnOne, Democracy, Oligarchy). The `group_type` discriminator is implicit in the contract address; there is no polymorphic `update_commitment` dispatch, no `group_type` field in storage, no `UpdateByType(u32)` VK kind. The §4.7.4 design-doc privacy benefit (selector hiding) collapses for the per-type architecture — chain observers learn the group type from the contract address itself, the same publicness class as `tier`.

error_codes.description: Numeric values of `Error` variants (`#[contracterror] #[repr(u32)]`). Per-type Democracy contract drops everything tied to multi-type dispatch (UnknownGroupType, GroupTypeMismatch), 1v1 (OneOnOneImmutable, Invalid1v1Tier), Oligarchy (MissingAdminRoot, AdminRootMismatch, Reserved22), v1 democracy artifacts (MemberCountMismatch, MemberCountOutOfRange, DemocracyInputMissing), and the unreachable UnknownVkKind=16 (VkKind is exhaustively matched). Adds InvalidThreshold per design v0.5.1 §4.7.6.

vectors.name: AdminOnly
vectors.note: create_group in restricted mode rejects non-admin callers (caller != admin after caller.require_auth)

vectors.name: InvalidThreshold
vectors.note: create_group rejects threshold_numerator < 1 || > 100; per design v0.5.1 §4.7.6

dropped_from_sep_xxxx.name: Reserved3
dropped_from_sep_xxxx.reason: previously Unauthorized; admin checks now use require_auth() which panics. Variant removed from the contract enum on the PLONK migration; code 3 is unallocated.

dropped_from_sep_xxxx.name: InvalidVkLength
dropped_from_sep_xxxx.reason: VK byte buffers are baked at compile time and the PLONK parser surfaces length errors as parse failures folded into InvalidProof; no caller-visible length error remains.

dropped_from_sep_xxxx.name: UnknownVkKind
dropped_from_sep_xxxx.reason: VkKind is exhaustively matched in update_vk; the variant was unreachable. Removed in PR #148 review (chunk 1 #4).

dropped_from_sep_xxxx.name: DemocracyInputMissing
dropped_from_sep_xxxx.reason: v2 has no count public inputs that could be missing

dropped_from_sep_xxxx.name: MemberCountMismatch
dropped_from_sep_xxxx.reason: v1 democracy artifact; v2 uses occupancy_commitment

dropped_from_sep_xxxx.name: MemberCountOutOfRange
dropped_from_sep_xxxx.reason: v1 democracy artifact; v2 internalises count constraints in-circuit

dropped_from_sep_xxxx.name: InvalidPoint
dropped_from_sep_xxxx.reason: PLONK verifier surfaces curve-decoding failures as parse failures folded into InvalidProof; no separate caller-visible code.

dropped_from_sep_xxxx.name: GroupStillActive
dropped_from_sep_xxxx.reason: deactivate_group was removed in postmortem #153 (frontrun); no active/inactive transition remains, so the guard is gone.

dropped_from_sep_xxxx.name: GroupTypeMismatch
dropped_from_sep_xxxx.reason: no polymorphic dispatch in single-type contract

tier.description: Tier index → tree depth → leaf capacity (per-tier bitmap slot count, NOT the per-tier group cap). Tier 2 capacity is retained for read-only membership verification of existing state, but create_group and update_commitment reject tier 2 until a real d11 quorum circuit lands. Per-tier slot capacity is the total in-tree slot space (cumulative-joins ceiling per design §4.1's tombstone-permanence rule). Pinned by `tier_capacity()` in the contract; orthogonal to the cross-group `MAX_GROUPS_PER_TIER` cap (see `create_group_validation` rules).

max_groups_per_tier.description: Cross-group cap enforced by create_group: per-tier active-group count must be < MAX_GROUPS_PER_TIER, else TierGroupLimitReached. Per-tier slot capacity (32/256/2048) caps members within a single group; this caps the number of groups at each tier on the contract.
max_groups_per_tier.rule: GroupCount(tier) MUST be < 10000 at create_group; exceeded → TierGroupLimitReached

vk_kind_enum.description: Two VK kinds for this contract. No `UpdateByType(u32)` because group_type is constant (Democracy). Membership VK is reused across create + verify_membership; Update VK is used only by update_commitment. (`deactivate_group` was removed in postmortem #153 — see docs/postmortem-deactivate-group-frontrun.md.)

vectors.name: Update
vectors.note: v2 democracy update circuit per design §4.7.3. The 6 actual public inputs (positions 1-6) include 5 wire-supplied scalars and 1 contract-supplied scalar (threshold_numerator, read from current.threshold_numerator at verify time per §4.7.6).

vk_kind_enum.ic_ordering_canonical: MUST match the v2 democracy circuit's allocation order. Any drift between client prover and contract verifier on this ordering produces silent verify failures. Pinned per design §4.7.6.

create_group_validation.description: Per-call validation in `create_group`. Single-type contract — no per-type dispatch. Threshold must be present (this is Democracy) and in [1, 100].
rules: tier ∈ {0, 1}; tier 2 and out-of-range values → InvalidTier
group_id MUST NOT already be assigned to a live or inactive group; collision → GroupAlreadyExists
commitment and occupancy_commitment_initial MUST be canonical Fr encodings (Fr::from_bytes ∘ Fr::to_bytes round-trip); non-canonical → InvalidCommitmentEncoding
GroupCount(tier) MUST be < MAX_GROUPS_PER_TIER (10000); exceeded → TierGroupLimitReached
if RestrictedMode is true: caller MUST equal Admin, else AdminOnly (caller.require_auth() runs first)
proof MUST verify under the membership VK at `tier` against the supplied (commitment, epoch=0) public inputs; failure → InvalidProof
proof_hash(proof) MUST NOT be in UsedProof storage; collision → ProofReplay

valid_vectors.semantics: simple majority (default per UI; non-strict — ties pass per §4.7.6)

domain_tags.description: Field-element domain tags used inside Poseidon hash inputs. Pinned per design §10.1 (Phase-A blocker). Codified in the planned `src/circuit/domain_tags.rs` (in the prover repo, NOT in this contract — the contract verifies proofs but doesn't compute Poseidon hashes itself). The contract is value-agnostic to these tags; they're listed here for cross-implementation reference. Future Anarchy / 1v1 / Oligarchy contracts MUST use disjoint values for their leaf and occupancy domain tags.

occupancy_bitmap_fold.description: Per design §4.7.2, the occupancy bitmap is folded into Fr scalar field elements at BITS_PER_FELT = 252 bits per scalar. Each scalar packs 252 bitmap bits in little-endian order: bit i of the bitmap is bit (i mod 252) of scalar (i div 252). The high bits 252-255 of every scalar MUST be zero (Fr modulus is ~2^254.86, so the high 3 bits cannot all be 1). The contract is value-agnostic to the bitmap encoding (it sees only the 32-byte occupancy_commitment hash output); these vectors are reference for cross-implementation testing of provers against this contract.

scalar_count_by_tier.note: 256 bits / 252 = 2 scalars; 4 high bits of scalar 1 are zero-padded
2048 bits / 252 = 9 scalars; 220 high bits of scalar 8 are zero-padded

occupancy_bitmap_fold.bit_packing_rule: bitmap[i] occupies bit position (i mod 252) of scalar (i div 252), little-endian within the scalar. Bits 252-255 of every produced scalar MUST be 0.

tier_0_examples.description: Slot 0 active (creator-only state at create_group time)
Slots 0 and 1 active (after first member join, no tombstones)

tier_1_examples.description: Slot 252 active (first bit of scalar 1; the boundary case that wedges if BITS_PER_FELT is misimplemented as 256)
Slots 0 and 252 active (one bit per scalar; tests the cross-scalar boundary)
Slot 255 active (last bit of bitmap; bit position 3 of scalar 1)

tier_2_examples.description: Slot 2047 active (last bit of bitmap; bit position (2047 mod 252) = 31 of scalar 8)

high_bits_zero_assertion.description: Every produced scalar MUST have bits 252-255 equal to 0 (canonical-Fr requirement). Implementations MUST reject (or refuse to produce) any scalar where the top byte (BE) has any of the high 4 bits set.

membership_count_constraints.description: v2 internalises all count-derived rules into the circuit (design §4.7.3 step 3). The contract no longer reads `member_count` from storage on the update path. The values below describe what the v2 democracy circuit is required to enforce, expressed as bitmap popcount conditions; they are reproduced as test vectors so the inline `prove_democracy_v2_*` cases (in the prover repo) and the contract integration tests have a single source of truth.

popcount_constraints.rationale: preserves democracy-of-≥2 invariant; was contract-only in v1, now in-circuit per §4.2.1
matches the single-signer-subset proof shape from §4.2; multi-signer transitions are deferred to Phase F
implicit in the bitmap's fixed length; no explicit constraint required

configurable per group (§4.7.6); K is the number of signers in the proof. K=1 is the single-signer subset baseline (§4.2). Non-strict >= means threshold=50 with m_old=4, K=2 (200 ≥ 200) passes — natural reading of 'simple majority = at least half'.

single_signer_examples.note: single-signer subset hits the limit at popcount_old ≥ 3

single_signer_max_m_old_by_threshold.comment: For K=1, max popcount_old supported is floor(100 / threshold_numerator)

update_commitment_public_inputs_wire_for.description: Wire encoding of `UpdateCommitmentPublicInputs`. Single Democracy variant (no discriminated union — the contract is Democracy-only). Encoded as a Soroban `#[contracttype]` struct. Five wire-supplied scalars; the verifier consumes a 6-public-input vector at proof verification — the 6th input (threshold_numerator) is supplied by the contract from current.threshold_numerator.
groth16_public_inputs: threshold_numerator (from current.threshold_numerator; NOT on the wire — design §4.7.6)
update_commitment_public_inputs_wire_for.vk_kind: Update (7 IC points: base + 6 public inputs)
canonical_encoding_rules: Every BytesN<32> commitment field MUST round-trip through Fr::from_bytes ∘ Fr::to_bytes; non-canonical encodings are rejected with InvalidCommitmentEncoding.
u64 epoch_old uses Soroban's native big-endian XDR encoding.
Public-input ordering for Groth16 verification is fixed: positions 1-5 from wire (in the listed order), position 6 from contract storage.

storage_layout.description: Storage entries written by the contract. Per-type Democracy means `group_type` is implicit in the contract address (not a stored field), and v2's occupancy_commitment replaces v1's member_count.

fields.name: epoch
fields.rule: starts at 0, increments by 1 per successful update_commitment

fields.name: timestamp
fields.rule: ledger timestamp when this state was recorded

fields.name: active
fields.rule: always true in current contract — `deactivate_group` was removed in postmortem #153, so no production code path sets this to false. Field + GroupInactive guard retained as defense-in-depth for any future re-introduction of deactivation.

fields.name: occupancy_commitment
fields.rule: Poseidon hash of the slot bitmap; canonical Fr encoding; replaces v1's member_count

fields.name: threshold_numerator
fields.rule: 1..=100; fixed at create_group, never mutated by update_commitment per design §4.7.6. Stored as u32 (matches contract); the value range fits in u8 but the storage type matches the wire-supplied tier type for consistency.

removed_vs_sep_xxxx.rationale: single-type contract; the contract address IS the discriminator
v1 democracy artifact; v2 commits to the bitmap via occupancy_commitment instead, hiding the count

data_keys.name: Admin
data_keys.note: contract admin; required for update_vk and set_restricted_mode

data_keys.name: RestrictedMode
data_keys.note: when true, only the admin may call create_group; toggled by set_restricted_mode (admin-only). Default false.

data_keys.name: VK(tier)
data_keys.note: membership-circuit VK for tier 0/1/2; 3 IC points

data_keys.name: UpdateVK(tier)
data_keys.note: v2 democracy update VK for tier 0/1/2; 7 IC points. NO `group_type` second key — single-type contract.

data_keys.name: History(group_id)
data_keys.note: rolling-window history of past entries; capped at HISTORY_WINDOW=64; pruned FIFO

data_keys.name: GroupCount(tier)
data_keys.note: active group count per tier; checked against MAX_GROUPS_PER_TIER (10000) in create_group. Monotonic increment-only since `deactivate_group` was removed (postmortem #153) — no decrement path exists; the cap is now a one-way ratchet. Operationally fine at testnet scale; if mainnet ever approaches 10k groups/tier, the architecture itself needs revisiting.

admin_entrypoints.description: Admin-gated entrypoints. All require admin.require_auth(); panic on unauth. There is no separate `set_admin` entrypoint — admin is fixed at __constructor.

vectors.name: update_vk

args.name: new_vk
args.rule: IC length matches `kind` (3 / 7); subgroup-valid; else InvalidVkLength / InvalidPoint

vectors.purpose: design §4.6 fingerprint-coordination triplet — rotate Membership or Update VK at a tier

vectors.name: set_restricted_mode
vectors.purpose: when restricted=true, create_group rejects non-admin callers with AdminOnly. Storage: DataKey::RestrictedMode (instance). Default value (key absent) is treated as false.

queries.description: Read-only entrypoints. None require auth. All return Result<_, Error>.

args.name: max_entries
args.rule: tail-truncated to most-recent `max_entries`; if max_entries ≥ history.len() returns all entries

vectors.name: verify_membership
vectors.note: returns Ok(false) on bad proof rather than InvalidProof — read-only verifier semantics

vectors.name: bump_group_ttl
vectors.note: permissionless TTL bump for long-lived groups

epoch_invariant.description: v2 keeps v1's monotonic-by-1 epoch rule. update_commitment increments stored.epoch by exactly 1 on success.

proof_replay_protection.description: Global nullifier scope. The nullifier is sha256(proof.a || proof.b || proof.c) over the 384-byte uncompressed proof bytes. Scope is global per contract: the same 384 bytes used at any state-changing entrypoint on any group consume the nullifier.
rules: Once a proof's bytes have been recorded by record_proof, any subsequent entrypoint call with byte-identical proof.a||proof.b||proof.c is rejected with ProofReplay.

TTL of UsedProof entries is LEDGER_BUMP (~30 days). After expiry the same proof bytes can be re-used; long-lived groups require periodic state advancement to keep nullifiers alive (mitigated in part by bump_group_ttl).

Pre-inclusion mempool replay is NOT closed by the contract-level nullifier — see existing notes in `contracts/sep-xxxx/src/lib.rs` for the circuit-level v2 (operation tag binding) that would close that.

tests_to_implement.description: Inline test names that the `#[cfg(test)] mod test` block in `contracts/sep-democracy/src/lib.rs` MUST cover. The `test_vectors_consistency` test loads this very file and asserts the contract's `Error` enum / tier mapping / IC-point constants match the values pinned above. Every other entry in this list maps 1:1 to a `#[test] fn` in the inline module.
